mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase borrowing costs for corporations and reduce the present value of future equity cash flows, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show positive mean returns during rate hike periods with statistical significance (p < 0.05), or rolling Rank IC exceeds 0.05 in the positive direction.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate increase exceeding 0.3 percentage points signals sudden labor market deterioration that triggers immediate risk-off positioning, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 65% of instances where UNRATE crosses the threshold, or the signal fails to generate a negative mean return with p < 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and builder confidence, driving positive equity returns over the next 60 trading days through earnings growth expectations.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict positive 60-day returns, with interaction term coefficient statistically indistinguishable from zero in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth exceeding 2% annualized during late-cycle expansions signals capacity constraints and inflationary pressure, prompting monetary tightening expectations that suppress S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth > 2% during late-cycle expansions yields positive 20-day forward returns with p < 0.05, or the signal loses predictive power when regime classification is applied.

mechanism_narrative: Monthly nonfarm payroll employment gains above 200,000 confirm robust economic activity and corporate revenue growth, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: PAYEMS readings above 200,000 are followed by negative mean 5-day returns with statistical significance, or rolling Rank IC remains below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: A core inflation index reading above 2.5% triggers expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL readings above 2.5% are followed by positive 60-day returns in > 60% of cases, or the threshold effect disappears in out-of-sample walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising real GDP and falling housing starts indicates a shift from residential to broader economic demand, supporting corporate earnings and generating positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The GDPC1-HOUST divergence interaction term shows no predictive power for 20-day returns, with out-of-sample R-squared below 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in industrial production during a post-shock recovery phase signals persistent supply-side weakness that undermines near-term earnings forecasts, causing negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines during post-shock recoveries yield positive 5-day forward returns with p < 0.05, or the regime-specific signal fails to outperform a baseline model.
